When we first launched apartment IQ, we pretty much assumed that any time a unit left a website, it was a lease, we knew that there was way more nuance than that.
But at that point, that was the logic that existed.
We've since refined it a little bit after doing a few audits of some of our major partners to see, you know, what is happening with units when they come off.
What we found is that the overwhelming majority of apartments, if they stay off for seven or more days, it's a lease that's converted.
If they go back within that seven day period, it was either a failed lease or it came down for a quick renovation, a paint job, what have you and make ready?
Uh And so that's kind of created this new way of looking at things where we'll start a unit in the applications area as soon as it comes off.
So unit leaves the website, it spends seven days in this application status on the eighth day.
If it is still off the website, we consider it a lease
